__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_0__: SaaS Revenue Concentration Risk: When Top Customers Hide Churn | Churn Lens
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_16_: When top customers hide churn risk: how to calculate revenue concentration (HHI), spot the whale-customer problem, and avoid buying a SaaS where one cancellation wipes out your returns.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_17_: A SaaS business with 3% monthly logo churn looks healthy on paper — until you realize a single customer accounts for 35% of MRR. Revenue concentration is the most common way churn risk hides during acquisition diligence. The headline number is fine; the underlying fragility is lethal.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_19_: Revenue concentration measures how much of your recurring revenue depends on a small number of customers. A business where the top 10 accounts make up 20% of MRR is diversified. A business where the top 10 accounts make up 70% of MRR is one negotiation away from a crisis.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_1__: How revenue concentration masks churn risk in SaaS acquisitions. Learn HHI calculation, the whale customer problem, and how to detect concentration before you buy.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_20_: For SaaS acquirers, concentration is distinct from churn — but the two interact. High concentration amplifies the impact of every lost logo. If your top customer represents 40% of MRR and they churn, your effective annual revenue churn isn't 3% — it's 43% overnight.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_22_: The cleanest way to quantify concentration is the Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI), borrowed from antitrust economics. For each customer, calculate their share of total MRR (as a percentage), square it, and sum across all customers:
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_24_: Higher HHI means more concentration. Here's how to read the result for a SaaS acquisition:
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_2__: SaaS Revenue Concentration Risk: When Top Customers Hide Churn
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_3__: How revenue concentration masks churn risk. Learn HHI calculation and how to spot the whale customer problem before you buy a SaaS.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_45_: For context: a perfectly diversified business with 1,000 customers paying identical MRR has an HHI of 1,000 (each customer = 0.1%, squared = 0.0001, × 1,000). A business with 10 equal customers has an HHI of 10,000. Most healthy SaaS targets sit between 500 and 1,500.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_46_: The Whale Customer Problem in Acquisitions
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_47_: A "whale" is any single customer representing more than 15–20% of MRR. Whales distort every metric a buyer cares about:
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_49_: — a whale on a 3-year contract suppresses monthly churn for 36 months, then explodes the annual churn number the year they leave.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_4__: SaaS Revenue Concentration Risk: When Top Customers Hide Churn
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_51_: — one whale expanding by $30K/month can mask 50 small accounts churning the same amount.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_53_: — a single upsell to a whale can generate a quarter of the year's new revenue, hiding weak product-market fit in the long tail.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_54_: Sellers rarely disclose whale risk proactively. They highlight the big logo as a "marquee customer" and a "land-and-expand success story." It's both of those things — and a single point of failure.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_55_: How to Detect Hidden Concentration Before You Buy
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_56_: During diligence, request the customer-level MRR export (not a summary). Then:
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_57_: Calculate the top-5, top-10, and top-20 MRR share.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_58_: If the top 5 exceeds 40%, flag it. If the top 10 exceeds 60%, it's a structural risk.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_59_: Check contract length for each whale.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_5__: How revenue concentration masks churn risk. Learn HHI calculation and the whale customer problem.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_60_: A whale on month-to-month billing is a ticking bomb. A whale on a 3-year contract with 18 months remaining gives you runway to diversify.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_62_: A whale acquired 3 months before the sale process is a red flag — sellers sometimes "buy" a big logo to inflate the headline number.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_64_: — don't accept a seller's "we're well-diversified" claim. See our
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_65_: deeper guide on customer concentration risk
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_67_: Want to measure concentration on a real target?
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_68_: Upload the customer MRR CSV and Churn Lens computes the HHI, top-customer share, and concentration-adjusted churn risk automatically.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_71_: The most dangerous SaaS profiles combine low headline churn with high concentration. The business looks stable because the whale hasn't left yet — but the probability of whale churn is uncorrelated with the historical churn rate. A whale that renews three times is not guaranteed to renew a fourth.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_72_: When evaluating a target, always compute
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_74_: : the monthly churn rate plus the implied monthly churn risk from whale exposure. If your top customer is 30% of MRR on an annual contract, that's an implied ~2.5% monthly churn risk layered on top of the base rate — even if they've never churned before.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_76_: If you proceed with a concentrated acquisition, protect yourself in the deal terms:
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_78_: — tie 15–25% of the purchase price to whale retention for 12–24 months post-close.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_80_: — structure 20–30% of the deal as an earnout contingent on the whale renewing.
__TR_saas-revenue-concentration-risk_82_: — get explicit reps on whale contract terms, renewal dates, and any known non-renewal signals.
